description: §20.8.1 — a `${...}` logic child inside `<outlet>` renders into the region (it is a markup-interpolation, not a default-logic mount-effect body). The live `[data-scrml-outlet]` region reads `Count: 0`. (Regression: the Wave-1a outlet branch walked children WITHOUT pushing the markup-parent context, so a program-level logic child was treated as default-logic mode and dropped its render slot — contradicting the 'children preserved' contract.)
rationale: §3.3: a `${expr}` inside a real markup element renders; the outlet region is such an element, so its logic child must get a render slot and show its value.
